text: Clean code is code that is easy to read and understand. One important aspect is using clear naming conventions. Variable and function names should describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice()` instead of `calc()`. Break your code into small, focused functions that each do one thing. This makes it easier to test and reuse parts of your program. Good code is also maintainable: when you return to it after months, or when someone else reads it, the logic is obvious. Writing clean code takes practice, but it saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
